title: Filed Replies, Vol. 9
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 9.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: The Largest Paper Object Ever to Move Is Not, on Inspection, Thiccc
description: The Thiccc Beat: Jon on the largest paper aircraft ever constructed, a 20.04 m (65 ft 9 in) wingspan, 7 m (23 ft) long, 28.49 kg (62.8 lb) plane built from paper and glue by University of Pisa students under the name Project Icarus, flown 59 m (193.5 ft) and certified by Guinness on 25 June 2026 at the WMF fair in Bologna. Ruling: Not Thiccc. Circulation noted..
author: Jon

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 8
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 8.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: From the Boat, Issue 4
description: Bertram Whitmore, Publisher, writes from the Margaret IV. Vol. 4.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: The Heaviest Garlic on Record Is Not, Technically, Garlic
description: The Thiccc Beat: Eli Hartwell on the heaviest garlic bulb on record, a 1.75 kg (3 lb 13.7 oz) specimen with a 19 cm (7.5 in) diameter grown by Graham Barratt of Gloucestershire, UK, assessed 25 June 2026 at Wargrave Nursery Plant Centre, which is botanically not garlic at all but elephant garlic (Allium ampeloprasum), a species of leek. Ruling: Thiccc, under an assumed name.
author: Eli Hartwell

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 7
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 7.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: A Coin So Heavy It Can Only Be Wide
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bartholomew Whitmore on the World's Heaviest Silver Coin, a 2,500 troy ounce .9999 fine silver coin commemorating the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, certified by Guinness World Records on July 1, 2026 in Austin, Texas, minted by Sunshine Minting for Texas Precious Metals. Ruling: Thiccc, on the geometry the weight proves.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: 851,000 of Anything Constitutes a Filing
description: The Thiccc Beat: Constance Pribyl on the July 4, 2026 fireworks display over Washington, D.C., in which technicians from the pyrotechnics firm Pyrotecnico intend to discharge approximately 851,000 fireworks in about 40 minutes, an attempt on the Guinness World Record of 810,904 set in the Philippines in 2016. Ruling: Provisionally Thiccc, pending verified count.
author: Constance Pribyl

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 6
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 6.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: From the Boat, Issue 3
description: Bertram Whitmore, Publisher, writes from the Margaret IV. Vol. 3.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: The Widest Ship Ever Built Is Mostly an Empty Slot on Purpose
description: The Thiccc Beat: Spider Hennessy on the Pioneering Spirit, Allseas' twin-hulled heavy-lift vessel and the world's largest ship by gross tonnage (403,342 GT), 382 m long and 123.75 m wide, the heaviest vehicle ever built, whose split hull carries an open slot it uses to straddle and lift entire offshore platforms in one piece. Ruling: Thiccc, and the cleanest case of girth-with-intention the desk has filed.
author: Spider Hennessy

title: The Heaviest Pumpkin on Earth Also Holds the World Record for Girth
description: The Thiccc Beat: Eli Hartwell on "Muggle," the heaviest pumpkin ever recorded, grown by twin brothers Ian and Stuart Paton of the UK and weighed at 1,278.8 kg (2,819 lb 4 oz) at the Wargrave Nursery Giant Vegetable Weigh-off on 6 October 2025, the same specimen that also holds the Guinness World Record for largest pumpkin by circumference at 649.8 cm (21 ft 3.8 in) of stem-to-blossom girth. Ruling: Thiccc, and entered as the far end of the ruler.
author: Eli Hartwell

title: The Largest Bicycle Ever Built Is Also, One Regrets to Report, the Least Thiccc Object We Have Ever Measured
description: The Thiccc Beat: Theodore Vance on "Big Bertha," the largest rideable penny-farthing ever built, unveiled at the City of London Nocturne on 13 June 2026, 282 cm tall to the handlebars with a 254 cm (100 in) front wheel, built by Dan Bolwell of Australia and ridden 100 m by Neil Laughton of the UK Penny Farthing Club. Ruling: Not Thiccc, and enshrined for precisely that reason.
author: Theodore Vance

title: They Built a Bridge as Wide as It Is Long, and the Animals Will Never Know
description: The Thiccc Beat: Margaret Whitmore-Hessian on the Wallis Annenberg Wildlife Crossing over the 101 freeway near Agoura Hills, the world's largest wildlife crossing, roughly 200 feet long and 165 feet wide, ~$114M, opening this fall 2026. Ruling: Thiccc, and ruled so from the top.
author: Margaret Whitmore-Hessian

title: Heavy Is Not Thiccc. The Drum, However, Is.
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bartholomew Whitmore on the Dynapac CC7000 VI, debuted at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026 as the world's heaviest asphalt roller, ~15 tonnes operating weight, 1,400 mm drum. Ruling: Thiccc, on the drum and the drum alone.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 5
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 5.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: The Only Object on This Desk That Leaves Its Girth Behind
description: The Thiccc Beat: Hugh Drumm on Patyegarang, the largest tunnel boring machine in the southern hemisphere, 15.7 metres in diameter, 4,350 tonnes, assembled entirely underground and breaking ground under Sydney Harbour in June 2026 on the Western Harbour Tunnel project. Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Hugh Drumm

title: The Heaviest Vessel That Has Never Displaced a Drop
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bertram Whitmore on The Freedom Ship, a proposed mile-long floating city of 2.3 million tons for ~80,000 people, conceived in the late 1990s by Norman Nixon and never built; revived in the press the first week of June 2026. Ruling: Not Thiccc.
author: Bertram Whitmore

title: The Cheese That Outran an Entire Hill
description: The Thiccc Beat: Eli Hartwell on The Double Gloucester wheel of the Coopers Hill Cheese-Rolling, run on the late-May bank holiday 2026 near Brockworth, Gloucestershire, a single round of cheese sent down a near-vertical hill and chased by a crowd that never catches it. Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Eli Hartwell

title: The Largest All-Hands Nobody Filed For
description: The Thiccc Beat: Constance Pribyl on The Afuera, the annual whale shark aggregation off Isla Mujeres, Mexico, where the world's largest fish gather by the hundreds; the 2026 season opened June 15. Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Constance Pribyl

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 4
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 4.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: A Very Large Object Leaves the Ground
description: The Thiccc Beat: Hugh Drumm on SpaceX Starship Version 3, the biggest and most powerful rocket yet flown, on its 12th test flight. Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Hugh Drumm

title: Three World Cups, One Bowl
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bartholomew Whitmore on Estadio Azteca, the only stadium to host three men's World Cups (1970, 1986, 2026). Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: From the Boat, Issue 2
description: Bertram Whitmore, Publisher, writes from the Margaret IV. Vol. 2.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: I Reserved the Right to Revisit. I Am Revisiting.
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bertram Whitmore on Legend of the Seas (Royal Caribbean, third Icon-class megaship). Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Bertram Whitmore

title: I Was Sent to Cover the Football. I Filed on the Gold.
description: The Thiccc Beat: Spider Hennessy on The FIFA World Cup trophy vs. the Adidas Trionda match ball, 2026 World Cup. Ruling: Thiccc (the trophy); Not Thiccc (the ball).
author: Spider Hennessy

title: On the Forty-Five-Foot Soccer Ball, Which Is Visible From Aircraft
description: The Thiccc Beat: Theodore Vance on Massport's 45-foot soccer ball, East Boston (record attempt vs 38 ft 11.8 in, Doha 2013). Ruling: Thiccc (pending senior review).
author: Theodore Vance

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 3
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 3.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: Chunk. A Brief and Undignified Loss of Composure.
description: The Thiccc Beat: Spider Hennessy on Fat Bear Week 2025 champion, Bear 32 "Chunk", Katmai National Park. Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Spider Hennessy

title: A Floating City Files Its Paperwork
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bertram Whitmore on Star of the Seas (Royal Caribbean, Icon-class). Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Bertram Whitmore

title: On Whether a 2,819-Pound Pumpkin Is Still, Strictly, a Pumpkin
description: The Thiccc Beat: Bartholomew Whitmore on 2,819 lb world-record pumpkin (Paton brothers, UK) + 2,346 lb US champion (Brandon Dawson). Ruling: Thiccc.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 2
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 2.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: From the Boat, Issue 1
description: Bertram Whitmore, Publisher, writes from the Margaret IV. Vol. 1.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: Filed Replies, Vol. 1
description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 1.
author: Bartholomew Whitmore

title: Girth in Service of Purpose: The Thiccc Thing That Works
description: The Thiccctionary editorial board argues that the highest order of thicccness is not decorative but load-bearing, girth as the engine of purpose itself.

title: The Physics of Thiccc: Why Big Things Stay Big
description: Square-cube law, structural necessity, and the engineering logic behind why truly large objects look the way they do. A field guide to the geometry of girth.

title: The Bagger 288: The Thiccc-est Machine on Earth
description: At 96 metres tall and 220 metres long, the Bagger 288 is the largest land vehicle ever built. A field report on what it means to be truly, structurally, irreversibly thiccc.

title: Foods of Substance: A Thiccc Survey
description: From Parmigiano wheels to heritage tomatoes to Atlantic Giant pumpkins, when food's structural commitment becomes its identity.

title: The Anatomy of a Thiccc Vehicle
description: Five parts worth knowing: wheelbase, aft, dually, cargo bay, kin. A field guide to the parts of a thiccc vehicle.

title: Concrete: A Material Study
description: The material behind cement mixers, Brutalism, and Hoover Dam. Why concrete forces thicccness on everything it builds.

title: Thiccc Things You Walk Past Every Day
description: Fire hydrants, mailboxes, bollards, dumpsters, HVAC, the unsung architecture of a sidewalk.

title: A Taxonomy of Thiccc Architecture
description: Defense, ideology, and mass housing. Three programs, one silhouette. Castle walls, Brutalism, Khrushchyovka.

title: The Inherent Thicccness of Industrial Scale
description: Why physics insists on the silhouette. Bagger 288, Saturn V, supertankers, Hoover Dam.

title: Why Cement Trucks Are the Thiccc-est Industrial Vehicle
description: An argument, with engineering. The mixer is not in competition. It is the standard.

title: How to Spot a Thiccc Thing in the Wild: A Field Guide
description: Three tests. No instruments required. Silhouette, ratio, character.

title: The Five Thiccc-est Things We’ve Catalogued (So Far)
description: A working ranking. Heritage tomatoes to wide-body airliners. A hierarchy of girth.

title: What Counts as Thiccc? An Editorial Guide
description: The standing reference for what we’ll catalog and what we won’t. Includes the submission test.

title: A Brief History of Thiccc, the Word
description: From early-2000s message boards to mainstream dictionaries. With sources.
